title: Agents and Infrastructure, Sam Lambert | Compile 26
summary: Sam Lambert, CEO of PlanetScale, discusses infrastructure changes needed for AI agents, demonstrating agents optimizing database operations including sharding and refactoring. He emphasizes safety mechanisms like branching, deployment controls, and rollback capabilities alongside infrastructure design patterns that prioritize human oversight.
writeup: Sam Lambert, CEO of PlanetScale, talks through the infrastructure changes databases need to support AI agents, with demonstrations of agents handling operations such as sharding and refactoring. The talk matters because letting agents act on production data raises questions about control and reversibility. Lambert centers safety mechanisms: branching, deployment controls, and rollback. He frames infrastructure design patterns around human oversight rather than fully autonomous action.
